name: Site Development & Grading Design
seoTitle: Site Development & Grading Design Services | Nopex Services
seoMetaDescription: Site development and grading design services engineered for proper drainage, code compliance, and buildable pad design on commercial and residential sites.
summary: Getting a site ready to build starts with grading that manages water, protects structures, and satisfies local code. We design grading and site development plans that balance earthwork efficiency with long-term drainage performance, coordinated with your architectural and MEP teams from the start.
description: Getting a site ready to build starts with grading that manages water, protects structures, and satisfies local code. We design grading and site development plans that balance earthwork efficiency with long-term drainage performance, coordinated with your architectural and MEP teams from the start.
tagline: Laying the groundwork for scalable, sustainable development.
process: Site Data Review — evaluate existing topographic survey and jurisdiction grading requirements
Grading & Earthwork Design — develop pad elevations, slopes, and cut/fill balance
Drainage Integration — coordinate grading with stormwater management design

faqs.q: What information do you need to start a grading design?
faqs.a: An existing topographic survey, the proposed building footprint, and any jurisdiction-specific grading ordinances.

faqs.q: Can you work from a survey we already have?
faqs.a: Yes — we design from client-supplied survey and geotechnical data rather than performing field surveys ourselves.

faqs.q: What information do you need to get started?
faqs.a: Send us your project scope, available design or site data, and any applicable code requirements through our Get a Quote form — we'll return a structured proposal and timeline for Site Development & Grading Design.

faqs.q: What's the typical turnaround time?
faqs.a: Turnaround depends on project size and complexity; most Site Development & Grading Design packages are delivered within [X] business days of receiving complete information.

faqs.q: Do you provide revisions based on reviewer or client comments?
faqs.a: Yes, a standard round of revisions is included as part of our QA/QC and client review process.

faqs.q: What file formats do you deliver in?
faqs.a: Deliverables are typically provided in AutoCAD Civil 3D and PDF, with Revit/BIM coordination available on request.

faqs.q: Is a licensed PE stamp available for this service?
faqs.a: Yes — stamped and sealed deliverables are available in all 50 states through our licensed PE partner network.

name: Stormwater Management & Drainage Design

summary: Stormwater requirements vary widely by jurisdiction, and getting them wrong can stall a permit application for months. We design detention/retention systems and drainage networks sized to meet local and state stormwater regulations, backed by supporting hydrology calculations.

Detention/Retention Sizing — calculate required storage and outflow control

name: 3D BIM Modeling

summary: A BIM model is only as useful as its accuracy and level of detail. We build architectural, structural, and MEP models to the specific Level of Development (LOD 100–500) your project phase requires, from early massing through construction-ready detail.

faqs.q: What LOD do you typically model to?
faqs.a: It depends on project phase — LOD 200–300 for design development, up to LOD 400–500 for fabrication and facility management.

faqs.a: Both — we can convert existing 2D drawings into a 3D model, or model directly from design intent.

faqs.a: Deliverables are typically provided in native Revit, Navisworks (NWD/NWC), or IFC, depending on your project's BIM execution plan.

faqs.q: Do BIM deliverables need to be stamped?
faqs.a: BIM models themselves are not typically stamped; if the underlying design requires a stamp, that's coordinated through our licensed PE partner network as part of the associated design service.
